Chapter 1

The Old Playbook Is Dead

The agentic era didn't accelerate the old B2B playbook — it ended it. Product lifecycles run in continuous deployment. Buyers consume content through AI intermediaries. GEO has replaced SEO. The funnel is no longer a funnel.

Pragmatic Remix: All 37 Boxes (Reframed) — Same boxes. Different operating tempo. Different outputs. Different skills.
📊 Download
PowerPoint Deck 📖 Download
PDF Chapter

The Core Thesis

"The PMM who survives this transition is not the one who learned to prompt faster — it's the one who understood what changed and why."

Product lifecycles run in continuous deployment. Buyers consume content through AI intermediaries. GEO has replaced SEO. The funnel is no longer a funnel. And the PMM who survives this transition is not the one who learned to prompt faster — it's the one who understood what changed and why.

Waterfall

1990–2005

Agile / Cloud

2005–2022

Agentic

2023–present

The Death of the Launch Cycle

Old Model

  • 3 releases per year
  • 8-week build → 2-week launch → 4-week measure
  • Messaging doc per release
  • Sales deck refresh per release
  • Analyst briefing per release
  • Demand gen campaign per release
  • Quarterly battlecard update

New Reality

  • Continuous deployment
  • 40+ production pushes per day
  • Durable positioning layer (evolves slowly)
  • Continuous CI layer (updates live)
  • Tactical activation layer (fires per feature)
  • Living competitive intelligence
  • Agent-readable structured content

The Content Crisis

90
seconds — that's how long it takes an AI agent to assemble the comparison matrix
that used to take your buyer six weeks to build.

The question is whether your product appears in it.

What Agents CAN Process

  • Structured comparison data
  • Third-party review rankings
  • Technical docs & APIs
  • Machine-readable pricing

What Agents SKIP

  • Narrative product pages
  • Video testimonials
  • Gated PDF case studies
  • Email nurture sequences

From SEO to GEO

SEO GEO
Optimize for Ranking on a list Citation in a synthesis
Compete on Keywords Entity authority
Build Backlinks Structured proof points
What matters Title tag Schema markup
Volume effect More content helps Better structure helps
Buyer sees Blue links Synthesized answer

Try this now: Open Claude, ChatGPT, or Perplexity and ask it to evaluate vendors in your category. That sixty-second exercise will tell you more about your GEO readiness than any consultant's report.

The Funnel is a Flywheel

— and the flywheel has an AI in the middle

Awareness
6 weeks → 90 seconds
Compressed by AI agents
Consideration
4 weeks → Minutes
Happens inside agent context
Decision
3 weeks → Days
Buyer arrives with AI dossier
Onboarding
Months → Weeks
AI agents handle setup
Retention
Annual review → Continuous
AI evaluates alternatives daily
Advocacy
Case study → Training data
Stories feed agent recommendations

Post-SaaS Economics

Per-seat licensing

DECLINING

Value = adoption
How many people use it?

Consumption-based

RISING

Value = usage
What volume are they driving?

Outcome-linked

EMERGING

Value = impact
What did the product deliver?

When shelfware disappears, the PMM's job extends deep into post-sale — helping customers find reasons to use 70 more seats, not just the 30 they're paying for.

The 10x PMM

1x PMM

Same job, better tools
  • 60% time on content production
  • Uses AI to write messaging docs faster
  • Generates more content with AI
  • Builds battlecards with AI assist
  • Same playbook, faster

10x PMM

Different job, different model
  • 60% time on strategy & judgment
  • Questions whether the artifact is right
  • Audits content for agent visibility
  • Builds living CI systems
  • New playbook, different problems

"The 10x PMM is not the one with the best prompt library. It is the one who has fundamentally rewired how they think about markets, products, and customers."

The Pragmatic 37, Remapped

Same boxes. Different operating tempo. Different outputs. Different skills.

Competitive Landscape

Quarterly battlecard update → Continuous signal monitoring → living CI system

Positioning

PowerPoint deck for sales kickoff → Structured data layer for AI agents + human narrative

Win/Loss Analysis

Post-mortem interviews quarterly → AI-analyzed deal recordings in real time

Content

Gated PDFs, blog posts, videos → Agent-readable structured assets + narrative pieces

Chapter 1 Deliverables

01

Old vs. New Playbook Diagnostic

Map your current GTM against its agentic-era equivalent. Identify gaps.

02

GEO Audit

Run 5+ evaluation queries across AI assistants. Score your visibility 0–5.

03

Content Survivability Assessment

Which of your top 20 assets are agent-readable, agent-adjacent, or invisible?

04

10x PMM Self-Assessment

Score across 10 dimensions. Design your 30-day upskilling plan.

Chapter Takeaways

  • The old playbook is dead. The agentic era didn't accelerate B2B marketing — it ended the model we've used for 20 years.
  • Launch cycles are obsolete. Continuous deployment means 40+ pushes per day. Your PMM rhythm must match product velocity.
  • Content has 90 seconds. That's how long an AI agent takes to build the comparison matrix that used to take buyers six weeks.
  • GEO > SEO. Optimize for citation in a synthesis, not ranking on a list.
  • The 10x PMM thinks differently. It's not about prompting faster — it's about questioning whether the artifact is even right.

The Future of Product Marketing
